title: Misanteca duartei
taxonomy: Misanteca duartei belongs to the family Annonaceae. It is a species of evergreen tree that is native to the Dominican Republic. The classification of Misanteca duartei also includes the genus name, Misanteca, which is commonly used for plants in the Annonaceae family. Further, it is classified as a member of the order Magnoliales. Additionally, some sources may group Misanteca duartei under the subclass Magnoliidae.

intro: Misanteca duartei, also known as West Indian bay tree, is a plant species native to the Caribbean region, belonging to the family Lauraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Misanteca duartei include misantol, a volatile oil that has been identified as the primary bioactive compound responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Misanteca duartei include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from respiratory problems to skin conditions.
